description: Central composite (CCD) response-surface DOE: a 2^k factorial plus axial and center points so a quadratic model can locate an optimum; returned in real units with a randomized run order.
rules: read-only — reads a JSON spec, writes a CSV; mutates no live service
seeded — the run order is reproducible from SPEC_JSON.seed
face='circumscribed' puts axial points OUTSIDE the (low,high) box; use 'inscribed'/'faced' to stay within range
usage: Set SPEC_JSON to a JSON file with keys: factors (required dict {name:[low,high]}), center ([int,int]), alpha (str, default 'orthogonal'), face (str, default 'circumscribed'), randomize (bool, default true), seed (int). Output: experimental_runs.csv under record_store.
limitation: Circumscribed axial points exceed the stated factor range, which may be infeasible/unsafe. When pyDOE3 is absent the porter writes an empty {} artifact.
